query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
Research complete. Here's the summary of KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell activity around June 2026:

## Key Findings

**Cumulative net selling (record):**
- **H1 2026 (Jan–Jun):** foreign investors net-sold **149.04 trillion won** (~$95–96.7bn) — a record
- **Through July 3:** cumulative net selling rose to **157.3 trillion won** (~$102.5bn)

**Monthly trend (accelerating selloff):**
- May: **−44.71tn won** | June: **−48.62tn won** (record month)
- Selling sharply accelerated from May onward, coinciding with a ~6% won depreciation

**Weekly/near-weekly detail:**
- May 7–June 11: 24-session selling streak totaling ~74tn won
- **June 12:** foreigners flipped to **net buying ~2tn won** (KOSPI +4.63% to 8,132.62)
- **Week ending June 15:** ~1tn won net buying (KOSPI reclaimed 8,500)
- **June 19–29:** 8 straight days of net selling
- **June 29:** record single-day net sell of **7.73tn won** (quarter-end rebalancing)
- Early July: KOSPI fell back below 8,000 on AI chip demand fears

**Context:** Despite the record selloff, KOSPI surged ~92–101% YTD. ~90% of selling was in Samsung Electronics and SK hynix. The KRX frames it as portfolio rebalancing/profit-taking, not lost confidence. Retail investors absorbed the flow (net-buying 104.8tn won). Brokerages expect selling to continue into H2 2026.

Most recent hard data: through July 3, 2026. Minor source discrepancies noted (148.32 vs 149.04tn for H1). Structured output has been delivered with all data points and sources.

key_facts: Foreign investors net-sold a record 149.04 trillion won on the KOSPI in H1 2026 (Jan-Jun); some outlets cite 148.32 trillion won for the Jan-Jun window.
Cumulative foreign net selling reached 157.3 trillion won (~$102.5 billion) through July 3, 2026.
June 2026 was the record monthly net sell at -48.62 trillion won; May was -44.71 trillion won. Selling accelerated from May onward.
June 29, 2026 saw a record single-day foreign net sell of 7.73 trillion won (quarter-end index rebalancing).
Week of June 12: foreigners returned as net buyers of ~2 trillion won (some reports ~1.7tn), ending a selling streak; KOSPI rose 4.63% to 8,132.62.
Week ending June 15: foreign net buying of ~1 trillion won as they absorbed retail selling; KOSPI reclaimed 8,500.
June 19-29: 8 consecutive trading days of foreign net selling.
May 7-June 11: 24 straight sessions of net selling totaling roughly 74 trillion won.
About 90% of first-half foreign selling was concentrated in Samsung Electronics (72.6tn won sold) and SK hynix (57.1tn won sold).
KOSPI surged ~91.9-101% YTD in H1 2026; the selloff is framed by KRX as rebalancing/profit-taking, not lost confidence.
Korean won weakened ~6% vs USD YTD, moving from ~1,483 to ~1,549 per dollar during May-June.
Retail investors net-bought 104.8 trillion won through July 3, 2026; foreign KOSPI ownership was 40.47% on July 3.
Brokerages expect foreign selling to continue into H2 2026; estimated additional selling potential ~260 trillion won if foreign ownership drops to 35%.
